what_it_does: Defines an HTML render-proof page for the transcript-viz archetype, loading shared surface runtime scripts, creating mock transcript/search records, and rendering direct, raw, grounded, and degraded constellation examples into page mounts.
observations: The document title identifies it as a transcript-viz constellation render proof with breadth marker #3.
It loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts plus shared piece and phone stylesheets.
Inline CSS forces block layout for the body and the four mount containers, explicitly overriding a loaded body display:flex rule.
The script fetches /dna/layouts.json and selects layouts.archetypes["transcript-viz"].
A local mount helper renders the archetype with DNA.renderArchetype and passes a DNA.org.constellation visual device.
The raw mock models a semantic search_sessions response with seven session results, scores, states, hit counts, titles, and snippets.
The direct mount injects a standalone constellation render centered in a max-width wrapper.
The grounded mock models a determine-style response with topic, asset, filter, no_fiction flag, claim count, themes, and claims.
The degraded mock copies the raw mock while changing mode_used to lexical and marking semantic unavailable with a reason.
standouts: It compares the constellation both as a direct organism render and inside the transcript-viz archetype shape slot.
It includes semantic, grounded-claims, and lexical-fallback data shapes in one proof page.
A file comment records a containment/full-width centering issue and notes that the host was fixed on the clone.
conventions: Uses a self-invoking async browser script to fetch layout data before rendering.
Uses mock data inline rather than loading fixture JSON.
Uses shared DNA runtime globals instead of module imports.
Uses terse mount IDs that correspond to the rendered cases: direct, raw, grounded, and deg.
Breaks from external body layout by applying a file-local display override.
concerns: The direct render uses innerHTML string concatenation with generated markup, which assumes DNA.org.constellation returns trusted HTML.
The page has no visible labels for the four rendered cases beyond their DOM IDs and comments.
The fetch and render path has no error handling if layouts.json or runtime globals are unavailable.
notes: A browser-only proof fixture that exercises transcript-viz constellation rendering across representative raw, grounded, and degraded transcript records.
questions: What does breadth #3 denote in this render-proof naming scheme?
Should the rendered cases be visually labeled in the page, or are DOM mount IDs sufficient for this fixture?
Is the direct organism render intended to remain part of the proof after the containment issue noted in the comment is resolved?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated render-proof purpose matches the file: it constructs a transcript-viz constellation proof page with direct, raw, grounded, and degraded examples.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a compact visual test page for validating how transcript-related records appear as constellations inside and outside the transcript-viz archetype.
novelty: The file pairs a direct organism render with archetype-contained renders and uses three distinct transcript data shapes to exercise the same visual system.
contribution: This file contributes a concrete transcript-viz proof fixture with representative mock records, degraded-state coverage, and a direct constellation rendering case that a fused system would keep as render-verification material.
summary_for_embedding: HTML render-proof page for the transcript-viz archetype that loads DNA surface runtime scripts and renders direct, raw search, grounded claim, and lexical-fallback constellation examples from inline mocks.
intention_for_embedding: It was for visually checking transcript-viz constellation behavior across several transcript record shapes and a degraded semantic state.
